idea-plugin.name: MockServer
idea-plugin.description:
Author, record, and debug HTTP/HTTPS mocks for MockServer without leaving your IDE.

MockServer lets you mock any system you integrate with over HTTP or HTTPS — services, REST or RPC APIs, and more — so you can build and test against simulated dependencies instead of real ones. This plugin brings the full MockServer toolset into IntelliJ IDEA and every other JetBrains IDE.

Highlights

  • Schema-driven expectation authoring — name a file *.mockserver.json and the editor validates it as you type, with autocompletion and hover documentation, using the same JSON Schema MockServer validates against. No language server, no extra setup.
  • Record real traffic into code — capture the requests MockServer proxied from a real upstream and save them as ready-to-use expectations, as JSON or as a Java DSL snippet.
  • Generate mocks from an OpenAPI/Swagger spec — turn the spec open in your editor into a full set of expectations in one action.
  • Live MockServer dashboard, embedded in the IDE — watch requests, inspect match results, and review active expectations in a dedicated tool window, without switching to a browser.

See the screenshots for record-to-code, schema authoring, and the in-IDE dashboard.

Everything it does

Server

  • Start MockServer (Docker) — launch the configured mockserver/mockserver container on the configured port with one click (the image tracks the plugin version by default).
  • Open MockServer Dashboard in IDE — embed the live dashboard in a right-hand tool window using the bundled JCEF (Chromium) engine, with Reload and Open-in-Browser buttons; falls back to your browser when JCEF is unavailable.
  • Open MockServer Dashboard — open the live dashboard in your default browser.
  • Reset MockServer — clear all expectations and recorded logs on the running server (confirms first).

Editor actions (operate on the active file)

  • Load Expectations Into Running Server — send the active file (a single expectation or an array) to the running server.
  • Save Recorded Expectations — retrieve expectations MockServer recorded from proxied/forwarded traffic and open them in a new tab, choosing JSON or Java DSL output.
  • Generate Expectations From OpenAPI Spec — send the active OpenAPI/Swagger spec (JSON or YAML) to MockServer and open the generated expectations.
  • Send Test Request — fire the ad-hoc HTTP request described in the active editor and open the response in a new tab.
  • Show Drift Report — fetch MockServer's mock-drift records (how real upstream responses differ from your stubs) and open a formatted report.
  • Find Requests by Trace — enter a W3C trace id or full traceparent value and open every received request carrying that trace as JSON.

Advanced

  • Upload WASM Module — pick a compiled .wasm custom-rule module and upload it, then reference it by name as a WASM body matcher.
  • List WASM Modules — show the WASM custom-rule modules registered on the running server.
  • MockServer tool window — a dockable panel that surfaces every action as a one-click button, grouped into Server, Editor, and WASM rows.
  • Settings — configure the Docker image, container name, and port under Settings | Tools | MockServer.

Requirements — a running MockServer instance at the configured port (Docker is optional, and only needed for the Start action). WASM actions require wasmEnabled=true on the server.

Links — mock-server.com for full documentation and client libraries, and the GitHub repository for source and issues.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
7.1.0 — first release; full IDE integration:

  • Expectation-file validation with JSON Schema completion and hover docs in *.mockserver.json files
  • Start MockServer via Docker, with a Docker-availability pre-check
  • Open the MockServer dashboard embedded in the IDE (JCEF) or in your browser
  • Load Expectations, Save Recorded Expectations (JSON or Java DSL), Generate From OpenAPI Spec, and Send Test Request from the active editor
  • Show Drift Report and Find Requests by Trace (W3C traceparent)
  • Upload and List WASM custom-rule modules
  • Reset the running server
  • Dockable MockServer tool window with one-click buttons grouped by Server / Editor / WASM
  • Settings for Docker image, container name, and port
  • UX polish: a marketplace icon, per-action icons and grouping in the Tools menu, a status line and section headers in the tool window, and a friendly "no server running" panel in the embedded dashboard

action.description: Opens the MockServer dashboard in your default browser
Shows the live MockServer dashboard inside the IDE using the bundled Chromium engine
Starts a MockServer container via Docker
Clears all expectations and logs on the running MockServer (PUT /mockserver/reset)
Sends the active editor file to PUT /mockserver/expectation on the running MockServer
Retrieves expectations recorded from proxied traffic and opens them in a new tab (JSON or Java DSL)
Sends the active editor's OpenAPI/Swagger spec to MockServer and opens the generated expectations
Sends the active editor's ad-hoc HTTP request to the running MockServer and opens the response
Fetches MockServer's mock-drift records and opens a readable text report in a new tab
Finds every received request belonging to a W3C trace id (or traceparent) and opens them in a new JSON tab
Uploads a compiled .wasm custom-rule module to the running MockServer (PUT /mockserver/wasm/modules)
Lists the WASM custom-rule modules registered on the running MockServer (GET /mockserver/wasm/modules)
